findings.evidence: Diff changes `private lemma orbitRel_eq_ker_of_exists_apply_eq` to `lemma`; the only new external use is `QuotientCovering.lean:56` in `apply_eq_iff_mem_orbit`.
findings.fix: Keep this helper private by moving the orbit-membership lemma into `Deck/Quotient.lean` next to it, or prove the new theorem from existing public quotient lemmas without exporting this helper.
findings.issue: `orbitRel_eq_ker_of_exists_apply_eq` was made public only to support the new file, exposing an implementation-level Setoid equality rather than the named deck quotient-cover API.

findings.evidence: `QuotientCovering.lean:64` calls `apply_eq_iff_mem_orbit (isRegular_iff_exists_apply_eq.mp hreg).2`, showing the public theorem itself needs the missing `IsRegular`-level characteristic lemma.
findings.fix: Add a scoped lemma such as `Deck.IsRegular.apply_eq_iff_mem_orbit (hreg : IsRegular p) : p e₁ = p e₂ ↔ e₁ ∈ MulAction.orbit (Deck p) e₂`, and use it in `IsRegular.isQuotientCoveringMap`; the raw helper can remain private.
findings.issue: The exposed orbit characterization requires the raw pointwise transitivity hypothesis, so users with `hreg : IsRegular p` must unfold through `isRegular_iff_exists_apply_eq` instead of using a characteristic regularity API.

summary: The main quotient-cover theorem has the right shape, but the PR exposes a low-level relation helper and does not provide the regularity-scoped orbit characterization that downstream users would naturally use.
